mission.title: vault: AWS backend stsAssume with "External ID"
mission.description: AWS backend stsAssume with "External ID". Requested by 13+ users.

steps.title: Check current vault setup
steps.description:
Verify your vault version and configuration:
```bash
vault version
```
This feature requires a working vault installation.

steps.title: Review vault configuration
steps.description:
Review the relevant vault configuration:
**Feature Request:**
Security improvement for AWS backend "External ID" parameter for stsAssume operation.

steps.title: Apply the fix for AWS backend stsAssume with "External ID"
steps.description:
This PR adds support for providing an external ID when calling `sts:AssumeRole` in AWS.
Related issue: #3790

## Motivation

It's mostly a security concern, referred to as the Confused Deputy problem. The AWS docs best describe why this is needed, especially when assuming a role in a foreign account.
Docs are here: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/id_roles_create_for-user_externalid.html

## TL;DR

* add support for providing `external_id` when creating a new AWS role.
* add

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected.
Confirm the feature described in "AWS backend stsAssume with "External ID"" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
This PR adds support for providing an external ID when calling `sts:AssumeRole` in AWS.
Related issue: #3790

## Motivation

It's mostly a security concern, referred to as the Confused Deputy problem. The AWS docs best describe why this is needed, especially when assuming a role in a foreign account.
